subject: Anthropology
title: Indigenous Rights
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Indigenous Rights.

hook.text: An oil company wants to drill on sacred land. The tribe has lived there for ten thousand years. Who wins?

buildup.text: Anthropologists increasingly advocate for Indigenous peoples' rights to land, language, and self-determination.

discovery.text: Colonialism stripped Indigenous groups of territory and identity. Activism and law now slowly restore both.

twist.text: Anthropology itself has a colonial past. Studying Indigenous peoples once served empire, not justice.

climax.text: Today, many anthropologists work with Indigenous communities as partners, not subjects — a hard-won shift.

punchline.text: Studying people means standing with them.

quiz.question: How has anthropology's relationship with Indigenous peoples changed?
options: From studying them as subjects to working with them as partners
